video_title: Common Council Regular Meeting, Monday, February 9th, 2026

segments.raw_content:
[00:05:10] our regular meeting. Thank you everybody
[00:05:14] for joining us. Oh, wait. I got to go.
[00:05:18] [laughter]
[00:05:19] Thank you for joining us. Um,
[00:05:22] let's please stand for the pledge of
[00:05:24] allegiance and then we're going to keep
[00:05:25] standing for the
[00:05:32] To the flag to
[00:05:33] >> the flag of the United States of America
[00:05:36] and to the republic for which it stands.
[00:05:39] One nation under God, indivisible, with
[00:05:42] liberty and justice for all.
[00:05:48] >> Pastor,
[00:05:55] good morning.
[00:05:57] Thank you for the invite this morning.
[00:05:59] It's a pleasure and an honor to be here.
[00:06:01] And before I pray, I'd just like to say
[00:06:03] a few words. My name is Pastor Dan. I
[00:06:05] pastor a church on 750 James Street. And
[00:06:09] it's a multicultural diverse church. I
[00:06:11] came to Syracuse in 1993. As I got out
[00:06:15] of prison, a voice led me to Syracuse. I
[00:06:19] needed change. I was an addict. I
[00:06:22] struggled to even
[00:06:24] find the words of my own to even why I
[00:06:27] would even bother to live or even have
[00:06:28] hope to live in my life. But I've
[00:06:31] learned to follow that voice even to
[00:06:33] this day. I'm a father of four, a
[00:06:36] grandfather of four, and I've had many
[00:06:39] journeys in over 33 years of servicing
[00:06:41] this community. We have served over two
[00:06:44] million meals to this community. We've
[00:06:46] served thousands and thousands of
[00:06:49] turkeys of some of you have probably
[00:06:51] heard us on TV.
[00:06:53] But our purpose is to serve and to
[00:06:55] follow a voice. My challenge to you is
[00:06:57] what is in your voice?
[00:07:00] What are you representing? What voices
[00:07:02] are you following?
[00:07:04] Are they voices of peace, of grace, of
[00:07:07] mercy? Are they voices of change?
[00:07:12] These are all important things. But if
[00:07:14] we can distill them down into the
[00:07:16] simplest things,
[00:07:19] it's a voice where we need to know that
[00:07:22] we can make a difference.
[00:07:25] That is your quest.
[00:07:28] Thank you, city council, for this
[00:07:29] invite.
[00:07:31] And I pray,
[00:07:33] my prayer is that you hear the voice.
[00:07:36] The Bible says in Proverbs that there is
[00:07:38] a voice crying on the streets. The Bible
[00:07:41] also says that his voice is a still
[00:07:44] small voice. Maybe his voice is the
[00:07:47] quietest in the room, but if we could
[00:07:50] all find some time to listen and hear
[00:07:53] what he has to say,
[00:07:55] maybe we'll have a better
[00:07:58] chance of actually creating real change.
[00:08:02] I understand change.
[00:08:06] It's been the most difficult challenge
[00:08:08] of my life is to change.
[00:08:11] It's brought me through 20 years of
[00:08:13] technology, brought me to starting my
[00:08:16] own companies, being a CEO of a software
[00:08:20] company, and today I still work. I sell
[00:08:24] industrial environmental equipment to
[00:08:26] help our environment. I'm reaching the
[00:08:28] 200 million mark of sales.
[00:08:32] So, I believe in change. I believe in
[00:08:34] purpose. I believe in hope. I believe
[00:08:36] that every person in this city has an
[00:08:38] opportunity to change. But it's a matter
[00:08:40] of whether or not you make a choice to
[00:08:41] hear the still small voice. Father, I
[00:08:45] thank you for this council. I pray that
[00:08:47] you would unclog every ear. That they
[00:08:50] would be able to hear your voice,
[00:08:54] follow it, listen to it, have care, have
[00:08:57] concern. And I thank you, Father, in
[00:09:00] Jesus name. Amen.
[00:09:02] >> Amen.
[00:09:02] >> Amen.
[00:09:04] Okay.
[00:09:08] All right. Thank you, pastor.
[00:09:11] So, going right into our agenda, we
[00:09:14] have,
[00:09:16] let's see, public hearings.
[00:09:18] >> Do the roll call.
[00:09:19] >> Oh, I'm sorry. Can you please do the
[00:09:21] roll call?
[00:09:21] >> Sure, Madam President. Councelor
[00:09:23] Codwell,

[00:09:55] >> So moved. Second.
[00:09:56] >> Thank you. Motion's passed to approve

agenda_descriptions:
Application & Agreement — To and with the NYS Department of Labor, Hazard
Abatement Board, for the 2026-2027 Occupational Safety Department of Labor,
Hazard Abatement Board, for the 2026-2027 Occupational Safety and Health
Training and Education (OSH T&E) Program, in an amount not to exceed $17,421,...

Bond Ordinance — Authorizing the issuance and sale of bonds to defray the cost of
the 2025/2026 Dig Once Streetscape Repair Capital Improvement Project. Total
amount not to exceed $245,000.

Bond Ordinance - Authorizing the issuance and sale of bonds of the City of Syracuse
to defray the cost of the DPW 2026/2027 CHIPS Reimbursable Sidewalk Project, to
complete the sidewalks and roads, as detailed in Schedule “A”, which may be
adjusted as needed to receive full NYS DOT reimbursement.

Permission — (revocable) To Axon Enterprises, to construct, own, operate and
maintain a Citywide Deployment of Axon Enterprises, Inc., License Plate Reading
(LPR) Cameras (Solar Powered) in the City Right of Way, under a Master Services
Agreement executed with the Department of Police.

Agreement — With Dan Groves DBA Olympic Tree and Land Management, to
conduct Basic Chainsaw Operations Training at Heath Park, on behalf of Onondaga
Earth Corps for two (2) days during the week of February 16". There is no cost to the
City.

Local Law — Of the City of Syracuse, to opt-in the Good Cause Eviction Law, New
York Real Property Law Chapter 50, Article 6a, and adjust the number of units that
define a small landlord to owners of rental properties containing no more than one
dwelling unit.

Agreement — With AXON Enterprises, Inc, for the purchase, installation and
maintenance of twenty-six (26) License Plate Reading cameras, on behalf of the
Police Department, effective March 1, 2026, for the period of sixty (60) months, as
detailed in the attached Master Service Agreement.

segments.title: Item 39 Axon LPR Contract -- Monto Explains Revocability and Flock Transition
segments.summary: Councilor Monto introduces item 39 (Axon LPR camera contract) and addresses public attendees concerned about surveillance. Explains council spent months reviewing the contract, which is 'highly and easily revocable' if Axon violates terms. Flock cameras are being held (not cancelled) to prevent service lapse during vendor transition. Item passes unanimously despite public disruption.

political_signal.description: Councilor Monto publicly defends Axon LPR contract as carefully negotiated with strong revocation clause, while acknowledging surveillance technology concerns

processing_notes: Connects to b75K4ejDfY4 Joint Public Safety & Econ Dev committee meeting Feb 6 where Axon contract was discussed in detail. Public attendees disrupted proceedings; council had to repeatedly ask for order.

segments.title: Items 40-43 UKG Pro Payroll Modernization -- Council Hesitation and Forward Vote
segments.summary: Councilor Williams introduces items 40-43 (Mosaic Consulting/UKG Pro payroll modernization) and delivers public statement acknowledging months of council concern about allocating additional funds. States council is 'at a point that we need to move forward' and hopes the administration can 'prove us wrong.' Items pass unanimously after extended disruption forced adjournment and constituent removal.

political_signal.description: Councilor Williams publicly signals council's reservations about payroll modernization spending while voting to proceed, putting administration on notice to deliver results

processing_notes: Connects to qUjV1ZYa2nY Finance Committee Feb 3 where payroll modernization was discussed in detail. Williams' statement is notable -- expresses hope administration 'proves us wrong.' UKG Pro contract is with Mosaic Consulting Group (RFP #25-266), 18-month implementation.
